title: COPD follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

referral.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped COPD follow-up identity, breathlessness, exercise, exacerbation, inhaler, smoking, function, oxygen, oedema, spirometry, oxygen assessment, clinician-decided plan, smoking, rehabilitation, vaccination, referral, and arranged-follow-up fields.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.137–1.2.140 and Table 6 — follow-up and review

follow_up.text: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped cough, sputum, worsening breathlessness, and sputum-colour review as documented features without asserting an exacerbation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Assessed-negative fields, acute red flags, chest auscultation, respiratory rate, accessory muscles, percussion, previous records, generic clinician plans, fixed follow-up, PRN wording, and the lowercase typo in the legacy history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis confirmation, treatment choice, medicine dosing, oxygen interpretation, acute disposition, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
